AMGN.rationale: At ~15x forward adjusted earnings ($20.60 midpoint guidance) with a 3.1% dividend yield and Phase 3 MariTide trials launched, AMGN trades at a meaningful discount to large-cap pharma peers despite 19% revenue growth and a Q4 earnings beat — the risk-reward skews mildly positive but soft EPS guidance, $60B debt, and MariTide's Phase 2 bone density headwind vs LLY/NVO cap conviction.

2025 adj EPS guidance midpoint $20.60 implies forward P/E ~15.1x — cheap vs large-cap biopharma peers at 18-22x, with 3.1% dividend yield providing downside support
MariTide Phase 3 MARITIME program launched Mar 5 with two pivotal trials, partially de-risking the obesity pipeline after underwhelming Phase 2 (20% weight loss, bone density concerns)
Debt/equity 8.0x ($60.4B total debt) remains elevated from Horizon acquisition but operating margin recovering (13.3% Q1 → 19.1% Q3 2024) as integration progresses
Stock down 3.6% from Mar 10 high of $327 on pharma tariff fears (Mar 25 news), creating entry point at $311.55

AMZN.rationale: The margin inflection thesis has accelerated beyond prior expectations — FY2024 operating margin reached 10.8% with net income doubling to $59.2B — and the 21% pullback from February highs on tariff-driven sentiment weakness has compressed P/E to 34x, the cheapest it has been in this thesis cycle, creating attractive risk-adjusted reward over a one-year horizon.

P/E diluted compressed to 34.4x from 62.2x a year ago and 47.2x at prior decision, as earnings grew 90% while stock appreciated only 5.5%
Stock down 21% from $242 Feb 4 peak to $190 on March 31 — tariff-driven selloff disconnected from record-strong fundamentals
Consistently positive news sentiment averaging ~0.6 over prior 60 days with 550+ articles scored, indicating market narrative remains constructive despite macro fears

AMGN.final_explanation: AMGN offers mild upside at ~15x adjusted earnings: Q4 beat, Horizon integration healing, and Phase 3 MariTide launch offset by soft EPS guidance, heavy debt, and competitive obesity headwinds.

AMZN.final_explanation: AMZN's margin inflection thesis continues to strengthen — FY2024 operating margin hit 10.8% with net income doubling YoY — and the recent 21% pullback from February highs on tariff fears has compressed P/E to 34x, creating the most attractive entry point in the thesis lifecycle.

IBM.final_explanation: IBM's Q4 2024 results and 2025 guidance are genuinely improved, but the stock's 79% one-year rally has pushed valuation to ~39x TTM P/E — pricing in an AI-driven growth inflection that 5%+ revenue guidance and 9% operating margins cannot yet justify.

JPM.final_explanation: JPM's 12.5% selloff from ATH has improved risk-reward from the stretched levels I flagged in prior decisions, but with macro uncertainty elevated (tariffs, recession risk, Dimon's own warnings), the stock at 13x P/E is fairly priced rather than compellingly cheap — no clear edge in either direction.

MCD.final_explanation: MCD is a quality franchise trading at a stretched valuation — near ATH at 27.4x trailing P/E despite flat-to-declining EPS, leaving risk/reward mildly unfavorable for new money over the next year.

MSFT.final_explanation: MSFT's valuation has meaningfully compressed from 38x to 31x P/E while fundamentals continued accelerating — the market has derated a high-quality AI compounder without fundamental cause, creating the most attractive risk/reward entry in 2+ years of prior decisions.

NVDA.final_explanation: NVDA's fundamental story has strengthened while its valuation has compressed significantly — the prior view's main constraint (expensive valuation) is now substantially addressed, making the risk-reward more attractive for a ~1 year horizon.

SHW.final_explanation: SHW remains mildly unattractive: 33x P/E on flat revenue with housing headwinds, despite solid 14% EPS growth and improved balance sheet.

TRV.final_explanation: TRV's FY2024 earnings surge validated the prior thesis, but with consensus expecting a 21% EPS decline in 2025 and the stock up 39% in a year near highs, the risk-reward is now balanced rather than compelling — TTM P/E of 12.3x is misleading as forward P/E is ~15.6x on normalized earnings.

VZ.final_explanation: VZ's valuation re-rating from deep-value to fair-value has overshot while fundamentals deteriorate — paying 18.7x PE for 16.3% operating margins and flat revenue with competitive headwinds is unattractive risk-adjusted.
